# Introduction

This is a book created from markdown and executable code.

See @knuth84 for additional discussion of literate programming.

::: {.theorem #thm-hi}

## Fermat's First Theorem

This extension has been written by a teacher who hopes that students read the course notes...
:::

Please look back to @thm-hi

::: {.lemma #lem-bye}
This extension has been written by a teacher who hopes that students read the course notes...
:::

::: {.exercise #exr-next}
This extension has been written by a teacher who hopes that students read the course notes...
:::

::: {.example #exm-the}
This extension has been written by a teacher who hopes that students read the course notes...
:::

## Acknowledgements

The author acknowledges support of the Institut Henri Poincar\'e (UAR 839 CNRS-Sorbonne Universit\'e), and LabEx CARMIN (ANR-10-LABX-59-01).

Also thank: John Rock, Yuri Sulyman, Desmos
